title: 法國柔道老師與台灣奧運柔道選手楊勇緯的相遇與交流
transcript:
J'étais pas dit du tout ce qu'on allait faire pendant ce séjour là. Mais il y avait un truc que tu savais ce qu'on allait faire. Il y avait un truc que je savais, c'est qu'on devait rencontrer Yang Yung-wei. Un modèle olympique que je suis depuis depuis une paire d'années maintenant, depuis 2018 je le suis. Donc du coup tu sais que je suis donc je travaille dans le lycée, pour ça qu'on peut faire les vidéos là-bas et en plus de ça, mon travail principal c'est de prof de judo. Donc j'ai mes deux clubs, Colombes et les Belles et Charme. Et quand tu étais venu en France, on avait fait une vidéo à 1h30 du matin ou 2h du matin, on avait bu un petit peu et tu m'as dit Lucas, c'est maintenant que tu vas défier Yang Yung-wei. J'ai dit 'Ah bon?' Et comme j'étais un peu bourré, j'ai fait 'Allez!' 

(Lucas speaking Chinese) Yang Yung-wei, ni hao. Wo shi judo xuan shou Lucas. Yao bu yao PK? PK? PK? 

(Narrator in Chinese) Knowing how much Lucas admired Yang Yung-wei, our team decided to make use of Lucas's time in Taiwan and try to get them to meet. So we contacted Yang Yung-wei directly on Instagram. And he actually said yes to our proposal. After discussing back and forth with his agent, we waited till his game overseas was over and met him in a French restaurant in Taichung. I've never seen Lucas this nervous. 

(Lucas in French) Alright Lucas, you must have been waiting for this day to come. Yeah, since the day I arrived I've been waiting for this. I'm a bit too excited right now. Don't mess up my polo like that, bruv. I need to look good for Yang Yung-wei. Taiwan's first judo athlete to win an Olympic medal. To be the first of a country is incredible. I was so excited I couldn't stay put. Just to see him on TV and then here today in person. 

(Lucas in French) I know you've been looking forward to this day. And now he is finally here. Thanks bruv. No worries. That's when I see him appear in front of me looking all cool. Yeah! Yeah! Go on then. I just feel like hugging him right away. Bring it in. Let's uchikomi. Oh! The guy is basically a superstar. But he said hi and was super friendly. None of that. He was really humble and all. And you can tell that he was happy to be here. It was really cool. You almost won at the Olympics. Oh you watched my game! Of course he did. How do you say 'thank you' in French? 'Merci'. 'Merci'. Nice. My god. He is so happy. Let's sit down then. Yes. You look so cool. Lucas also dressed up for you. Very nice. Yeah. This is my best polo shirt. Look at those muscles. Do you want to touch it? Wow. How do you say 'handsome'? 'Beau gosse'. 'Beau gosse'. Oh well thanks. You can tell him too. His shirt is also really cool. You are very pretty? Huh? Pretty is for girls. You should say 'handsome'. Yeah handsome. Okay okay. Lucas, let's first try Yung Wei's favorite dish. He is from Houli. So this is what he wants to eat the most when he is homesick. Oh. Yeah he flies over a few days before to get used to the timezone. So this here. Taiwanese will eat this for either breakfast or lunch. Like my dad he loves to eat their fried noodles. And it's always for breakfast. You mean you wake up and eat this right away? That's crazy. You haven't had this in a while huh? Yeah it has been a while. Nice then. Two are super spicy and one just a bit. So we don't know who has what. Yeah exactly. This is a bit spicy innit? Hmm. I guess he got the super spicy one. I feel like you can go on chopping wood after eating this. You defo feel strong after eating this in the morning. I think I got the spicy one as well. You both got it then. It's really red yeah. You want to switch? I know you guys don't eat very spicy. Innit? French people aren't great with spices? Do you want mine? Sure I... Thank you. It actually tastes fine without the spice. Uh. Well bummer then. It was nice. Mine was really spicy. I was like no wonder he fights like he does at games. Have a bit of noodles before and you'll defo win. Let's talk about your own rituals before a game. I talked to Lucas about it. Every time he goes to a game he has his own little ritual. Always a pack of strawberry candies in my bag. And I'd eat them after the game. I think we can let you try a few. Yeah. He always eat those. These are tasty! Have you tried them before? I've tried them before. I would buy these when I'm overseas. Wow. The way his eyes went bright when he saw the candies. It was just like when I first saw him. He was so excited. And once I told him the candies were for him. He was like 'Ohhhh'. Of course. This is so great. You can only find those overseas. Uh. This pack is for you. Oh thank you. What about you and your rituals? Out of the house I always go pray at the temple before I leave. So that everything goes well. And I also have this lucky charm thing. Would I call it that? Yeah. It's like an omamori. This here. And where is this from? It was from a Japanese friend. He gave it to me. Oh there is your name as well. So I always have this on me. And on the back is 'Must win'. Right. That is the motto of judo. He said this is the way of judo. Exactly why I have it on me. It was really cool. There was really an exchange there. He shows us his favorite Taiwanese dish. And I show him my favorite French one. Ooh la la. This is a really local French dish. Our chef Benjamin made it for us. He also invited us here so thank you very much. I just saw how he prepared it. It was crazy. There was a lot of cheese. So there are potatoes, bacon and onions. Uh. And a bit of white wine cream. And half a reblochon. Alright let us dig in. Wow. It is very hot. This is really good. For sure. I'm so happy, I haven't had this for so long. Even in France we don't eat this well. Don't think we can eat this during training. Yeah. Fo sho. For sure. I remember he really liked it. He even packed it up to go. So I guess he really liked it. Yeah for sure. Then he took out his medal. Wow that was a really touching moment. Lucas has a small wish. He would love to see your medal. Can he? Sure. We got a small surprise for you. I'm so excited I've never seen one. Wow~ it is huge. Wow~ Bruv. He is about to cry. I'm almost there. Oh. You take it. No I can't. Or do you want a gold one? I want to touch it as well. I really want to touch it. Wow it's really heavy. The weight is no joke. Let's not. I really want to touch it though. You ready? You good? Wow bruv. When they put it around your neck you. There aren't many Olympic medal winners out there. You get a chance only once every four years. He is about to cry now. Leave me alone. It was a rare opportunity and I even got to touch it. And I know how much he went through to achieve this. People can't even imagine. All the work and wounds that are behind this. So it was a really touching. I can only imagine. How hard it must have been to be able to get to this. He says he's feeling emotional because he knows how hard you must've been working to achieve this milestone. People might just see a medal and nothing else. But... Although we couldn't communicate properly. We understood each other that day. There was a spark. Thank you. I'm going to cry as well. Oh. Alright. Let's go practice judo. To cheer up a bit. Okay. Let's go. We will first do warmups. He first taught us what he usually does for warmups. They were pretty standard moves. But watching a judo star do it still hits different. It might seem the same to most people. But I could tell from details in his movements. The reason why he was such a good athlete. Whew~ I can't get up. Okay. It's really warm now. You good Lucas? Great warmup. Okay. Now we'll do warmups the French way. And see what is different. I mean I think I'm already warmed up enough. So then it was my turn. I showed them how I led warmups in my classes. I like my students to have fun when in class. So we'd do sets like leapfrog. I know it seem childish. But even as a 30 year old I still find it fun. And this snake-crawling a well. You gotta crawl between the legs as fast as you can. It is great for cardio training. Alright. The next one is also fun. It trains your grip. You prepare like this. Uh. Each person does it 5 times. Wow Lucas feels so different than Lucas. You can do it. Okay. 5, 6, 7, 8. Okay. We're done yeah. Let's go home then. After the sets we were properly warmed-up. We sweated so much. I remember you too. But he looked just fine. Then we went on to muscle training. Now we're going to the scariest part of training. I feel like this might be my last video ever. I'm doomed. So... we're doing that? That's scary. Uh? Uh? Having a good grip is very important in judo. I remember the interesting thing about these were the fabric felt really similar to that of our judo costume. Can you do this bruv? You're allowed to use your feet. Oh. Come on. Can I do it like this? You need to hit it real quick. Oh! This training is specifically made for judo. Instead of doing usual muscle training. It's even better to do it like this. Find a smilier fabric and practice directly with it. Have you seen this in France before? Not really no. Then we went on to your training back in France. We did this push up game. How long they doing it for? A whole minute. Is anyone counting? Nope. Alright then we start over again. After we trained together. We had a randori planned. Now we're moving to the long-awaited part. Too bad though. We cannot let them play against each other. They're very different in weight. We've got someone else. To play against Lucas. Yeah. Thank you. What's your name and your weight? For ten years. Uh. And... You a bit shy? Ehm I also weigh 81. Kilos. Yeah okay. Let's start then? Okay. Alright, let's go. So cute. I wasn't fully focused at first. We were still training. And suddenly we decided to fight. I was like 'Alright then'. There was even a proper points board. And Yung Wei as the referee, awesome. I know I'm not that well trained. I haven't trained properly in a really long time. Teaching and training is completely different. I remember secretly hoping that the game won't last for too long. Hajimi! Three minutes guys. Lucas! The game started. The boy was quite fit. And he was a leftie as well. Lefties are even harder to go up against. Aww how cute. I feel like it's just hugs. I think it's adorable. After about 30 seconds in I scored half a point. I wanted to score a full one and end it but no. So I had to keep going. I was like 'this is going to be so long'. Wow he is doing hiphop moves. Matte! Three minutes is more than enough. He said three minutes is enough. You can see me tiring out by the end. I couldn't breath properly anymore. title: 法國柔道老師與台灣奧運柔道選手楊勇緯的相遇與交流
transcript:
我從2018年就開始關注楊勇緯的比賽，他是一位奧運選手，我一直很欣賞他。這次來台灣，我有一個特別的機會能見到他。

我除了在高中工作，還是一名柔道老師，在兩個俱樂部教課。上次來法國的時候，有一次凌晨一兩點，我們喝了點酒，你突然跟我說：'盧卡斯，你現在就去跟楊勇緯下戰帖！' 我當時有點醉了，就答應了。所以我們團隊就決定要珍惜盧卡斯來台灣的機會，讓他們兩個認識。我們在IG上主動跟楊勇緯聯絡，他竟然答應了！

跟經紀人溝通了一段時間後，我們等楊勇緯的比賽結束，約在台中一間法式餐廳見面。我從來沒看過盧卡斯這麼緊張。他來的時候很熱情地跟我們打招呼，一點架子都沒有，整個人超謙虛的，也感覺得出他很開心見到我們。真的超棒的！

我們介紹了楊勇緯最愛吃的家鄉味炒麵，這是他想家時最想吃的食物。台灣人早餐或中午都可以吃，像我爸爸就很喜歡吃他們的炒麵，而且都是早餐的時候去吃。我問楊勇緯是不是很久沒吃了，他說很久沒吃了。我吃了一口，覺得有點辣，應該是大辣。我說吃完這個，一大早應該可以直接去砍木柴了，你一定會很有力量。我猜我應該也是大辣，你們兩個都是大辣。法國人不吃辣，所以我們交換了。楊勇緯說其實不加辣就很好吃，我心想'可惡'。

楊勇緯也分享了他的賽前儀式：出發前會去廟裡拜拜，也會帶一個日本朋友送的幸運物，上面有他的名字和『必勝』。他說這就是柔道的精神，所以我一直帶著。那天真的有交流到，他介紹他喜歡的台灣小吃，我介紹我喜歡的法國菜。我們的廚師Benjamin幫我們準備了非常道地的法國菜，他也很歡迎我們來這裡，所以非常謝謝他。我看到他怎麼準備的，非常非常的可怕，起司非常非常的多。裡面有馬鈴薯、培根和洋蔥，還有點白酒奶油醬跟半個Reblochon起司。真的超級好吃，我好開心，好久沒吃這個了，我們就算在法國也沒吃這麼好。楊勇緯說這個在降體重時應該就不能吃。我記得他吃得很開心，最後還打包帶走了，所以應該是很喜歡。

接著楊勇緯拿出他的奧運銀牌。哇，那一刻我真的感動到不行。盧卡斯有一個小小的願望，他想看你的銀牌，可以嗎？我超興奮的，第一次看到。哇～超大的，哇～兄弟。他快哭了。我真的要哭了。你拿著。不要不要我。還是你要拿金牌？等一下我自己也想摸啦，我超想摸的。哇很重，這個重量沒有在開玩笑。不要。不行我還是想摸一下。準備好了嗎？你可以嗎？哇兄弟。掛在脖子上的時候直接。世界上有多少人可以這樣贏到奧運獎牌？每四年才有那麼一次機會。要哭了，要哭了，開心。不要鬧我啦。真的超難得，而且我竟然還可以摸到它。我知道這需要多少的努力才可以得到。一般人絕對沒辦法想像。為了這獎牌花了多少時間，受了多少傷。所以看到的當下真的很感動。我完全能體會。這獎牌背後的辛苦。他感動的原因是因為他知道你有多辛苦，才拿到這個銀牌。大家可能看到就說一面獎牌嘛，有什麼大不了的。但真的，我們雖然語言不通，但那當下我們完全能理解彼此，只有運動員才能懂。謝謝。我也要哭了。好的。那我們先去練柔道吧，換一下氣氛。好，Let's go。

我們快速換了衣服。穿上柔道服後，站在那個場地裡又是另一種感動。能跟他一起吃飯已經很難得了，沒想到還能這樣跟他一起練習。我那時想說：'好，現在要認真了。' 我們來到台體大，因為我們終於可以跟楊勇緯一起訓練。他一開始先教他平常做的暖身，都是蠻常見的一些基本動作。但看著一位明星選手做還是不一樣。一般人可能看起來都差不多，但我從他一些動作上的細節就能看出來，為什麼他可以這麼厲害。呼～我起不來啦。很熱。盧卡斯還好嗎？很棒的暖身。接下來我們來換法國的暖身，看看哪裡不一樣吧。其實感覺不需要啦。接著就換我來介紹，我在法國教課時會帶的暖身。我很喜歡讓學生上課時玩得開心，所以會安排像是跳馬背的動作。雖然很幼稚啦，但像我都30歲了，也還是覺得很好玩。這個爬行的動作也是，用最快的速度從每個人腿間爬過去，可以訓練一下心肺耐力。好的。下一個也蠻好玩的，是用來訓練你手指的力量。先像這樣準備好。每個人輪流做五下。哇跟盧卡斯差很多欸。一定可以。OK。五個、六個、七個、八個。好。結束了吧。我們都熱起來了。流超多汗的。我記得你也是啊。他看起來倒是還好。接著我們就來到肌耐力訓練。那我們現在來換最可怕的階段。可能就是我們這一輩子最後一部影片了。怎麼辦？這是要去那裡嗎？好可怕。嗯？柔道裡的握力非常重要。我記得這些布條很有趣的是，它的材質跟我們柔道服非常像。與其做一般的肌耐力訓練，還不如像他們這樣，直接找最接近柔道服的布料練習。你有在法國看過這種嗎？像這樣的還真的沒有。接著我們練了你在法國會做的訓練。我們做了伏地挺身猜拳。這樣要做多久？一分鐘。有人在計時嗎？沒有。好，那現在開始重算。訓練結束了之後，他們安排了比賽時間，我們有一個亂取。那我們現在要進行大家最期待的階段。那比較可惜的是，我們不能讓他們兩個比賽，因為量級差太多。我們找到一個朋友，可以跟盧卡斯比賽。耶～謝謝。你叫什麼名字？你幾公斤？我練十年了。嗯。然後。有點害羞是不是？額，我也是81公斤的。量級。好的。OK。那我們開始好不好？好。好的。Let's go。我一開始還沒太進入狀況，原本還在練習而已。結果突然就說要來比賽了。我想說：'喔，好。' 旁邊甚至有正式比賽的那種計分板。還有楊勇緯當裁判，酷斃了。我自己知道我狀況不理想，我已經很久沒有認真訓練了。教課跟訓練是完全不一樣的兩回事。我那時候還偷偷想說，希望這場比賽不要比太久。Hajimi！三分鐘，三分鐘。盧卡斯！比賽開始了。那個大男生真的蠻壯的。而且他還是個左撇子。跟左撇子比又更難一些。哇，好親切的感覺。其實就是擁抱。我覺得蠻可愛的。大概30秒後我就先拿了個半勝。我本來是想直接得一勝的，結果只得了半勝，所以只好繼續比。我想說：'天啊，這場一定會很久。' 哇，他在跳嘻哈欸。Matte！三分鐘就很夠了。他說三分鐘很夠。看得出來我比到後面越累。我開始越來越喘。另外個大男生還一副沒事。我想說我們一定會比超級無敵久。Hajimi！我還是有好好撐完最後幾秒，所以結果平手了。你那時還叫我們比延長賽。怎麼可能。我真的不行了。但那個大男生是很棒的對手。那非常謝謝楊勇緯歡迎我們。盧卡斯你有說什麼嗎？這是我最後一個字。沒事。沒有沒有，看起來OK。可能看不出來，但我很開心。聽到我有跟楊勇緯一起練柔道，很多朋友都超嫉妒我的。他們都說：'你要好好享受喔！' 現在回想還是覺得，真的很不可思議。柔道就是這麼神奇。我們不需要語言上的溝通。當然會有比賽啊什麼的。但我們非常尊重對方。比完後都會好好地鞠躬道謝。每次都是個很棒的經驗。這次我也絕對不會忘記。我們下次見，掰掰。我最期待的就是這一天。我知道來台灣會做很多瘋狂的事情。我知道你們準備了很多企劃。但身為柔道選手和老師，我就迫不及待跟楊勇緯見面。
summaryText:
這部影片記錄了法國柔道老師盧卡斯（Lucas）與台灣奧運柔道選手楊勇緯在台灣的相遇與交流。盧卡斯自2018年起就關注楊勇緯的比賽，對他非常欣賞。在一次酒後，盧卡斯萌生了向楊勇緯下戰帖的想法，而他的團隊也積極促成了這次會面。

他們在台中一間法式餐廳首次見面。盧卡斯對這次會面感到非常緊張和興奮，而楊勇緯的謙遜和友善也讓他印象深刻。在餐桌上，他們進行了有趣的文化交流。楊勇緯分享了他最愛的家鄉味炒麵，並解釋了台灣人早餐或午餐都會吃這道菜。盧卡斯嘗試後覺得很辣，並開玩笑說吃完這碗麵就能去砍柴了。楊勇緯也分享了他的賽前儀式，包括去廟裡拜拜和佩戴一個寫有他名字和「必勝」的日本幸運符。盧卡斯對此感到非常感動，認為這代表了柔道精神。

隨後，他們也品嚐了盧卡斯介紹的法國傳統菜餚——焗烤馬鈴薯（Tartiflette），這道菜由他們的廚師朋友Benjamin精心準備，裡面有馬鈴薯、培根、洋蔥、白酒奶油醬和Reblochon起司。大家對這道菜讚不絕口，楊勇緯甚至打包帶走，但他也幽默地指出這道菜不適合在減重期間食用。

影片中最感人的時刻之一是楊勇緯拿出他的奧運銀牌。盧卡斯看到獎牌時情緒激動，他深知這枚獎牌背後所付出的無數努力、汗水、傷痛和時間，這是一般人難以想像的。他小心翼翼地觸摸著獎牌，表達了對楊勇緯的敬意和理解。儘管語言不通，但他們透過柔道和共同的運動員精神，產生了深刻的共鳴。

午餐後，他們前往柔道訓練場。盧卡斯換上柔道服後，感到一種不同的興奮。楊勇緯帶領他們進行了日常的熱身訓練，包括一些常見的柔道基本動作和特殊的握力訓練。盧卡斯從楊勇緯的動作細節中，領悟到他之所以如此厲害的原因。隨後，他們進行了一場友誼賽（randori），由楊勇緯擔任裁判。由於體重差異，盧卡斯與一位學生進行對抗。比賽中，盧卡斯雖然奮力拼搏，但最終因體力不支而平手。他坦言自己很久沒有認真訓練，但這場比賽仍是一次難忘的經驗。盧卡斯強調，柔道是一種神奇的運動，它超越了語言的隔閡，讓不同文化背景的人們能夠互相理解和尊重。這次與楊勇緯的交流，對他而言是一次非常寶貴且難以忘懷的經歷。

translations.en:
## A French Judo Teacher's Encounter and Exchange with Taiwanese Olympic Judoka Yang Yung-wei

This video documents the encounter and exchange between Lucas, a French judo teacher, and Yang Yung-wei, a Taiwanese Olympic judoka, in Taiwan. Lucas has been following Yang Yung-wei's matches since 2018 and deeply admires him. After a night of drinking, Lucas had the idea to challenge Yang Yung-wei to a match, and his team actively facilitated this meeting.

They first met at a French restaurant in Taichung. Lucas was very nervous and excited about the meeting, and Yang Yung-wei's humility and friendliness left a deep impression on him. At the table, they engaged in interesting cultural exchanges. Yang Yung-wei shared his favorite hometown dish, fried noodles, explaining that Taiwanese people eat it for breakfast or lunch. Lucas tried it and found it spicy, jokingly saying that eating it would give him enough strength to chop wood. Yang Yung-wei also shared his pre-competition ritual, which includes praying at a temple and carrying a lucky charm from a Japanese friend, inscribed with his name and the words 'must win.' Lucas was deeply moved by this, seeing it as a representation of the spirit of judo.

Subsequently, they also tasted a traditional French dish, Tartiflette, introduced by Lucas. This dish, prepared by their chef friend Benjamin, contained potatoes, bacon, onions, white wine cream, and Reblochon cheese. Everyone praised the dish, and Yang Yung-wei even took some home, though he humorously noted it wasn't suitable for weight-cutting during training.

One of the most touching moments in the video was when Yang Yung-wei presented his Olympic silver medal. Lucas became emotional upon seeing the medal, understanding the immense effort, sweat, injuries, and time invested behind it—something ordinary people could hardly imagine. He carefully touched the medal, expressing his respect and understanding for Yang Yung-wei. Despite the language barrier, they connected deeply through judo and their shared athletic spirit.

After lunch, they headed to a judo training hall. Lucas felt a different kind of excitement as he put on his judogi. Yang Yung-wei led them through his daily warm-up routine, which included common judo movements and unique grip-strength exercises using fabric strips resembling judogi lapels. Lucas observed the details of Yang Yung-wei's movements, understanding the reasons behind his exceptional skill. Following this, they engaged in a friendly sparring session (randori), with Yang Yung-wei acting as the referee. Due to a significant weight difference, Lucas sparred against a student. Although Lucas fought hard, he eventually tied due to exhaustion. He admitted that he hadn't trained seriously in a long time, but the match was still an unforgettable experience. Lucas emphasized that judo is a magical sport that transcends language barriers, allowing people from different cultural backgrounds to understand and respect each other. This exchange with Yang Yung-wei was a truly valuable and memorable experience for him.
